Engineering is the highest form of Art

… and it isn’t even close.

The items below are all exquisite pieces, each for completely different reasons. But each one a game changer in their own domain.

It’s difficult to get across the level of effort that was poured into each of these but they represent millions of hours of hard work, from the most competent people a society can muster.

This is what par excellence looks like in the domain of exquisite hardware.

However… the age of exquisite might be ending.

It’s not crazy to consider that the high water mark of exquisite hardware is already behind us?

In the future we might be much more focused on the unit economics and the machine that makes the machine, rather than product itself.

This will fundamentally change what society builds, and how such articles are perceived.

You might be forever telling kids “they don’t make things how they used to” and this tike it might be true.

Not everywhere, but in enough places that it’s recognised as a generalised industrial trend.

Paschen’s law: 
V_b = (B p d) / [ln(A p d) - ln(ln(1 + 1/γ))]

Pull the plunger on a syringe next to a live Tesla coil and watch physics lose its mind.
The instant pressure drops, that p d term collapses; breakdown voltage craters, the field ionizes the rarefied air, and violet plasma arcs flood inside the barrel like lightning bottled in glass.

No more jumping to the needle. Just pure glowing discharge channels forming in vacuum.
This is the exact reason vacuum tubes, neon signs, and Crookes tubes exist.

Last year, we invested in @Monumental_Labs.

They use AI and robotics to reduce the cost of stone fabrication by 90%. 

Construction tech is our game and while I think what they are doing with art and sculpture fab is amazing, the bull case for ML imo was doing building scale construction with stone. Essentially, reviving a dormant material type for type I construction to compete directly with steel and concrete.

Before investing, I had to answer a question that's been nagging me for years: exactly when and why did we stop building with natural materials like stone? Where did beauty in our built environment go to die?

It's mostly always been an economic story.

I’m using a two-engine setup lately. Three.js handles the real 3D space and depth, then I pass that depth info into p5.js to draw. The right side looks 3D, but it’s actually all flat 2D drawing. That’s the fun part. I can render the brush strokes in pure 2D so the lines feel more hand-drawn, while still keeping proper depth and overlap.
In short, Three.js does the space. p5.js does the drawing feel.

Karateka, released in 1984 for the Apple II by Jordan Mechner (while he was a Yale student!), stood out as a groundbreaking title for its era. My biggest achievement as a student was getting out of bed in time for the majority (not all) classes...

The innovations included pioneering rotoscoped animation, where Mechner filmed himself performing karate moves on video, traced the frames by hand, and digitized them frame-by-frame for ultra-fluid, lifelike character movements.

It was also one of the first games to use cinematic storytelling scenes, inspired by Akira Kurosawa films, and silent movies: wordless cutscenes with cross-cutting (such as showing the princess in peril or the villain's reactions), dramatic pauses and tracking shots.

Mechner also had a sense of humor: The Apple II disk included an Easter egg: the game was single-sided but when you flipped the disk it would pay the game the full game upside-down and mirrored, causing players to call tech support in confusion.

Did you know that public land, especially National Grasslands managed by the US Forest Service, are the single largest blocks of relatively intact prairie left in the US and they’re ground zero for restoring the American Serengeti? 

The ‘American Serengeti’ is the nickname given to the Great Plains of North America (our grassland steppe stretching from Montana to Texas) with similar scope and scale of biodiversity as Africa’s Serengeti (bison, elk, pronghorn, wolves, griz). 

We have 20 National Grasslands (almost 4m acres) with public prairie also set aside in state and national parks as well. The biggest, which you can visit and dispersed camp on all year for free, are the Little Missouri National Grasslands (ND 1m acres) and Thunder Basin National Grassland (550k acres). 

Buffalo reintroduction is currently happening in Thunder Basin and the Little Missouri (and many other public National Grasslands) where, working with tribes & ranching families, Buffalo are being reintroduced or will be soon. Almost every black footed ferret reintroduction has taken place on American National Grasslands. 

National Grasslands conduct some of the largest prescribed burns in the federal land system, which restores fire grazing systems. Fire grazing, or pyric-herbivory systems are a combination of RX burns and targeted grazing that mimics the natural patterns that shaped the Great Plains before settlement. 

Back then, lightning would cause a fire, fresh grass would grow back, bison would heavily graze regrown patches, which would attract more grazing. This meant a mosaic would form and constantly shift with lightning fire burns, and patches of short, medium, and tall grass along the landscape. 

Federal land managers are also managing National Grasslands as what are called wildlife movement corridors, where on over 10m acres of American land (public and private) owners and managers are working together to benefit mule deer, pronghorn, sage grouse, and grassland birds.

Last year I taught a small army of college students how to draw site plans.  

If we have 100 people in the city who could sketch a remedial plan view and section, problems would start to solve themselves.

Urbanist schools no longer teach drawing, a major pedagogical failing, so it has to be done privately (by citizens) for free.  

I cannot stress this enough: the inability of the citizens to SEE a better solution allows both 1) copy-paste bureaucracies and 2) "hell-no!" no-change-ever crowds to run the city.  Absent vision, naysayers dictate tone, EVEN when they are not in power. Such is the machine.

 Visualizations are the solution. Drawings are the solution.

Vanguard-Cheetah XR7 // Salt x Diamond Corp.

The Vanguard-Cheetah XR7 combines the biological grace and speed of a cheetah with cutting-edge robotics. Its lightweight exoskeletal frame is built from a nano-alloy composite, providing durability without compromising agility. The internal musculature system, powered by synthetic myofibers, mimics the explosive power of a real cheetah, enabling bursts of speed up to 150 km/h in optimal conditions.

The XR7’s neural core is an advanced AI system capable of terrain mapping, autonomous decision-making, and target recognition. Embedded within its sleek design are modular tools for specialized missions, including life detection sensors and environmental samplers.

Advanced Features:

-HyperKinetic Actuators: These actuators provide near-instantaneous acceleration, allowing the XR7 to traverse rough terrain, climb obstacles, and perform agile maneuvers.
-Adaptive Skin Layer: A semi-transparent outer layer with embedded sensors that regulate temperature and detect environmental hazards.
-ThermoCore Power Unit: A compact reactor that uses thermoelectric conversion to generate power from environmental heat sources, ensuring prolonged operation in remote regions.
-VisionNet Suite: A multi-spectrum vision system capable of detecting infrared, ultraviolet, and electromagnetic signals for locating hidden objects or lifeforms.
-Biomechanical Tail Stabilizer: A dynamic tail that enhances stability and maneuverability during high-speed chases or sudden directional changes.
Mission Applications:

-Search and Rescue: The XR7 can navigate disaster zones to locate survivors, deliver supplies, and map hazardous areas.
-Environmental Monitoring: Equipped with biosensors, it tracks wildlife movements, measures air and soil quality, and identifies illegal deforestation or poaching activity.
-Security and Surveillance: Its stealth capabilities and rapid mobility make it ideal for patrolling large facilities or borders.
-Scientific Exploration: The XR7’s ability to cover vast terrains quickly makes it an asset in mapping ecosystems, studying migration patterns, and conducting biodiversity assessments.

Legacy:
The Vanguard-Cheetah XR7 is a testament to Salt x Diamond Corp.’s vision of engineering solutions that coexist with nature. Though its sleek, cybernetic appearance evokes technological superiority, its core mission is deeply rooted in preserving and protecting the natural world.

The XR7’s tagline, "Speed for Survival," underscores its dual purpose as both a defender of life and an explorer of the unknown.

古典的な幾何学的錯視の例。（a）ミュラー=リヤー錯視。上下の水平線分は同じ長さであるが、下の方が長く見える。（b）エビングハウス錯視。リングの中の円は左右同じ大きさであるが、大きい円のリングに囲まれた左の円よりも、小さい円のリングに囲まれた右の円の方が大きく見える。（c）ポンゾ錯視。Λや＜の頂点に近い方に置かれた対象が大きく見える。具体的には、2本の水平線分は同じ長さだが、上の方が長く見える。2つの円は同じ大きさだが、左の方が大きく見える。（d）ポッゲンドルフ錯視。2つの斜線は一直線上にあるが、右の斜線の方が上方に変位して見える。（e）ツェルナー錯視。垂直より反時計回りに45度傾いた黒い線は互いに平行だが、交互に傾いて見える。交差する短い線との交差角度の過大視の現象である。（f）へリング錯視（湾曲錯視）。水平線分が曲がって見える。上の線分は上に凸、下の線分は下に凸に見える。（g）ミュンスターベルク錯視。白と黒の正方形の列を図のようにずらして配置し、列の境界に線分を描くと、図ではそれらは水平であるが、交互に傾いて見える。本図のように線が灰色の時は、カフェウォール錯視（Café Wall illusion）と呼ばれることが多い。（h）フレーザー錯視。垂直より反時計回りに45度傾いた仮想線に沿って短い斜線の列が描かれているが、列の傾きは短い斜線の傾きの方向に変位して見える。ツェルナー錯視とは逆の錯視である。（i）フレーザー錯視の渦巻き錯視。フレーザー錯視では、傾いて見えるのは線分であるが、傾いて見える対象を円状に配置した時に観察できる錯視である。具体的には、同心円が渦巻きのように見える。

For #ToyStory 4, instead of manually creating cobwebs for their Antique Mall environment, @Pixar created AI spiders which would weave realistic cobwebs for them like a real spider.   

You can see the red dots which are the AI spiders as they weave cobwebs in real-time. This allowed the team to quickly and easily add cobwebs to their environments in a realistic manner.  

Any cobwebs that required interaction with characters were created manually to ensure that they behaved as the animators expected.  

The AI spiders were created by Hosuk Chang, a Sets Extensions Technical Director at Pixar.
